MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-12-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS KARAPET BADALYAN AND ARAM VARDANYAN'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND DISMISS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS DENIED. A DEFENDANT MAY MOVE TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON OR BEFORE THE LAST DAY OF HIS OR HER TIME TO PLEAD. (CCP 418.10(A).) DEFENDANT'S MOTION WAS FILED AND SERVED AFTER THAT TIME HAD EXPIRED, AND HAS NOT MADE A SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE FOR THE COURT TO EXTEND THE TIME TO FILE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185 =302/JR

ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND DISMISS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS THE ACTION ON THE GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARA AROUSTAMIAN'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO LOS ANGELES SUPERIOR COURT AND REQUEST FOR PLAINTIFF'S PAYMENT OF DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY'S FEES OF $1,560.00 AND THE COSTS OF THE TRANSFER. DEFENDANT AROUSTAMIAN'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO LOS ANGELES SUPERIOR COURT IS DENIED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 395(A). THE COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS A BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO DEFENDANT AROUSTAMIAN. THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT IS THE PROPER VENUE FOR THIS ACTION BECAUSE THE PARTIES AGREED TO ADHERE TO THE CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY AND "THE COUNTY WHERE THE OBLIGATION IS INCURRED IS THE COUNTY WHERE IT IS TO BE PERFORMED." C.C.P. 395(A). DEFENDANT AROUSTAMIAN'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND EXPENSES IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976.(302/EHG)

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Department 206 for Short Cause Trial/Prove Up. Department 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6840.

Plaintiffs James D. Sumner and Anthony K. Moroyan appearing on their own behalf.
No appearance by or on behalf of Defendants Karapet Badalyan and Aram Vardanyan.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-10 dismissed without prejudice.
Court granted Plaintiffs' oral motion to dismiss without prejudice First cause of action and injucntion.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff James Sumner and Anthony K. Moroyan and against Defendants Karapet Badalyan and Aram Vardanyan as follows:

Principal $18,221.64
Pre-Judgment Interest $0
Attorney's Fees $0
Costs $710.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $18,931.64

Judgment signed in open court.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Assigned To Trial Court

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-18-2015 Set For Case Management Conference On Apr-22-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JAMES D. SUMNER, TEL. NO. 1-408-206-7720, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. SASHA BROWER OF SHAGHZO & SHAGHZO LP, TEL. NO. 1-818-241-8887 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JAMES SUMNER and ANTHONY MOROYAN'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Amending plaintiff's motion to include a claim for malicious prosecution based on a lawsuit filed in Armenia will cause significant time delay, trial expenses, and prejudice to defendants. Defendant Aroustamian was not even a party to the Armenian action. Furthermore, whether a lawsuit filed in Armenia lacked probable cause requires knowledge of Armenian law that is not required for the California State Bar. In the instant case, it is improper to burden this Court with a complaint that necessitates sufficient knowledge of Armenian law. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sasha Brower, Esq. of Shaghzo & Shaghzo Law Firm, T:818.241.8887, for defendant and moving party by Court Call;
James D. Summer, T:408.206.7720, plaintiff and opposing party appearin on his own behalf by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ARA AROUSTAMIAN's General Demurrer and Special Demurrers as to the Complaint's Third Cause of Action for Specific Performance and Fourth Cause of Action for Breach of Oral Agreement are overruled. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles Superior Court; Request For Plaintiff'S Payment Of Defendant'S Attorney'S Fees Of $1,560.00 And The Costs Of The Transfer
Off Calendar Oct-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sasha Brower, Esq. of Shaghzo & Shaghzo Law Firm, T:818.241.8887, for defendant and moving party by Court Call;
James D. Summer, T:408.206.7720, plaintiff and opposing party appearin on his own behalf by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ARA AROUSTAMIAN's Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles Superior Court And Request For Plaintiff's Payment Of Defendant's Attorney'S Fees Of $1,560.00 And The Costs Of The Transfer. Defendant Aroustamian's Motion to Change Venue to Los Angeles Superior Court is denied pursuant to C.C.P. 395(a). The Complaint adequately pleads a breach of contract cause of action as to Defendant Aroustamian. The San Francisco Superior Court is the proper venue for this action because the parties agreed to adhere to the Confidentiality Agreement in San Francisco County and "the county where the obligation is incurred is the county where it is to be performed." C.C.P. 395(a). Defendant Aroustamian's request for attorney's fees and expenses is denied. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JAMES D. SUMNER, TEL. NO. (408)206-7720, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. SASHA BROWER OF THE SHANGHZO & SHANGHZO LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (818) 241-8887 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS KARAPET BADALYAN and ARAM VARDANYAN's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND DISMISS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION is denied. A defendant may move to quash service of summons on or before the last day of his or her time to plead. (CCP 418.10(a).) Defendant's Motion was filed and served after that time had expired, and has not made a showing of good cause for the Court to extend the time to file. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/JR
